output.code:
# Let's open the provided files and inspect the text
import os, json, re, textwrap, sys, unicodedata, itertools, pathlib

paths = ["/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt", "/mnt/data/file-4LBCLG5rFma2KY6ccpdtsH-Q026.txt"]
existing = [p for p in paths if os.path.exists(p)]
existing

path = "/mnt/data/file-4LBCLG5rFma2KY6ccpdtsH-Q026.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:4000]

# Find segments inside curly braces {} including braces
curly_spans = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group(0)) for m in re.finditer(r"\{[^{}]*\}", text)]
len(curly_spans), curly_spans[:5]

# Find indices of all double quotes:
quote_positions = [m.start() for m in re.finditer(r'"', text)]
quote_positions[:10], len(quote_positions)

quote_pairs = [(quote_positions[i], quote_positions[i+1]) for i in range(0, len(quote_positions)-1, 2)]
quote_pairs

hadith_spans = [(start, end+1, text[start:end+1]) for start, end in quote_pairs]  # include both quotes
len(hadith_spans), hadith_spans[:2]

queries: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَأَخْبَتُوا إِلَى رَبِّهِمْ أُولَئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ سورة هود 23
مَثَلُ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ كَالْأَعْمَى وَالْأَصَمِّ وَالْبَصِيرِ وَالسَّمِيعِ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ هود 24
قلنا احمل فيها من كل زوجين اثنين وما آمن معه إلا قليل هود 40
أُذِنَ لِنوحٍ بِالنُجومِ أَرُبَّعَةَ أَزْوَاجٍ hadith

جابر بن عبد الله رواه البخاري نوح

output.code:
# Build combined citations list with indices from curly_spans and hadith_spans
citations_spans = []
for s,e,txt in curly_spans:
    citations_spans.append({"type":"Aya","start":s,"end":e,"text":text[s:e]})
for s,e,txt in hadith_spans:
    citations_spans.append({"type":"Hadeeth","start":s,"end":e,"text":text[s:e]})
# sort by start
citations_spans = sorted(citations_spans, key=lambda x: x["start"])
# Count citations
len(citations_spans), citations_spans

# Build the final citations list per the schema
# Correct verse texts from dataset (we can paste them as constants here for now)
verse_1123 = "إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ وَأَخْبَتُوا إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ أُولَـٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ"
verse_1124 = "۞ مَثَلُ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ كَالْأَعْمَىٰ وَالْأَصَمِّ وَالْبَصِيرِ وَالسَّمِيعِ ۚ هَلْ يَسْتَوِيَانِ مَثَلًا ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ"
citations = []
for entry in citations_spans:
    if entry["type"] == "Aya":
        # Determine if this is the first or second aya span by start index to assign reference
        # The first one corresponds to Hud 23, the second to Hud 23-24
        if entry["start"] == curly_spans[0][0]:
            corrected_text = verse_1123
            aya_ref = {"sura_name":"هود","sura_number":11,"aya_number":23}
        else:
            corrected_text = verse_1123 + " " + verse_1124
            aya_ref = {"sura_name":"هود","sura_number":11,"aya_number":23}
        citations.append({
            "span_start": entry["start"],
            "span_end": entry["end"],
            "span_text": entry["text"],
            "citation_type": "Aya",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": aya_ref,
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": corrected_text,
            "completely_invalid": False
        })
    else:
        citations.append({
            "span_start": entry["start"],
            "span_end": entry["end"],
            "span_text": entry["text"],
            "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": {},
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": "خطأ",
            "completely_invalid": True
        })
len(citations), citations[0]

def strip_markers_and_update(start, end, text_segment):
    # Given the content of the segment including its markers, return new start & end & stripped text
    # For curly braces segments: { ... } ; We'll strip the first char and last char if they are braces.
    # For quotes segments: " ... " ; We'll strip the quotes
    seg = text[start:end]
    if seg.startswith("{") and seg.endswith("}"):
        return start+1, end-1, seg[1:-1]
    if seg.startswith('"') and seg.endswith('"'):
        return start+1, end-1, seg[1:-1]
    # else no change
    return start, end, seg

citations_adjusted = []
for entry in citations_spans:
    s,e,t = entry["start"], entry["end"], entry["text"]
    s2, e2, stripped = strip_markers_and_update(s,e,t)
    citations_adjusted.append({"type":entry["type"], "start":s2, "end":e2, "text":stripped})

citations_adjusted

citations_output = []
for entry in citations_adjusted:
    if entry["type"] == "Aya":
        # Determine which one; mapping to corrected texts and aya_reference
        if entry["start"] == citations_adjusted[0]["start"]:  # first
            corrected_text = verse_1123
            aya_ref = {"sura_name":"هود","sura_number":11,"aya_number":23}
        else:
            corrected_text = verse_1123 + " " + verse_1124
            aya_ref = {"sura_name":"هود","sura_number":11,"aya_number":23}
        citations_output.append({
            "span_start": entry["start"],
            "span_end": entry["end"],
            "span_text": entry["text"],
            "citation_type": "Aya",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": aya_ref,
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": corrected_text,
            "completely_invalid": False
        })
    else:
        citations_output.append({
            "span_start": entry["start"],
            "span_end": entry["end"],
            "span_text": entry["text"],
            "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
            "is_valid": False,
            "aya_reference": {},
            "hadeeth_reference": {},
            "corrected_text": "خطأ",
            "completely_invalid": True
        })

len(citations_output), citations_output[0], citations_output[-1]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
